mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the broader macro backdrop.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-signal periods do not outperform baseline hit rate at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, labor market deterioration crosses a threshold that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from 4.9 to 5.0 vintage-to-revised in Jan 2008 snapshot
failure_condition: Triggered instances show SPX 20d forward positive-return rate above 55%, or the signal fails to beat the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate inversely (i.e., does not suppress positive rate materially) across two independent 3-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts (HOUST) decline exceeding 20% year-over-year signals a residential investment contraction that propagates through construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value=1006.0 in Dec 2007, down sharply from prior housing boom peaks
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following the trigger are positive in more than 50% of instances, or effect size is not statistically distinct from unconditional 60d return distribution (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO jointly confirm a synchronized labor-and-production contraction that single-series signals miss individually, marking recession onset and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM decline AND INDPRO MoM decline concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised sharply lower (114.2334 vintage vs 102.2448 revised) suggesting downside momentum in production data
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 60d returns not significantly more negative than instances where only one of the two series declines (difference-in-means p>0.10), indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated (above 3.5%) while CPIAUCSL inflation is simultaneously accelerating, the Fed's inability to ease due to inflation constraints creates a stagflationary regime that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.94% Jan 2008 amid CPIAUCSL near 211.68 elevated levels
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint condition, SPX 20d forward positive-return rate exceeds 55%, or the regime-conditional effect disappears (p>0.10) once controlling for the standalone FEDFUNDS trend signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trailing 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which is priced into equities as improving near-term earnings prospects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO trend and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS (retail sales) exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer demand, which precedes downward revisions to corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value 367368 below vintage_value 382906 in Jan 2008, indicating downward consumer spending revision
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 20d forward return rate above 55% or no statistically significant difference from unconditional base rate (p>0.10) across multiple subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is rising while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously being cut aggressively, this divergence signals that monetary easing is reactive to a deteriorating labor market rather than proactive, so short-horizon equity returns remain negative until the easing cycle matures, after which forward returns over a full quarter turn positive as rate cuts take effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising 3-month trend AND FEDFUNDS cut > 0.5pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to 3.94% by Jan 2008 amid UNRATE rising to 5.0% revised
regime_hint: recession risk regime with reactive Fed easing
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns during the joint-trigger regime are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power versus FEDFUNDS-only or UNRATE-only signals (partial R^2 improvement < 0.01).
